sourceMovie.title: Hotel Rwanda
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Hotel Rwanda for the same reason it broke through in 2004: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a history rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Terry George keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and history blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Glory
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hotel Rwanda clicked for you, Glory (1989) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and history, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Hotel Rwanda?
faq.answer: Try Glory, The Zookeeper's Wife, Emancipation—each shares DNA with Hotel Rwanda while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Hotel Rwanda?
faq.answer: Hotel Rwanda (2004) is best described as Drama, History, War. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Hotel Rwanda worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
